hero.name: Juan Sebastián Lozano Lamus
hero.title: Senior Product Designer | E-commerce Strategy
hero.subtitle: Design systems and conversion optimization specialist for Premium brands · Bogotá, Colombia

items.title: Kreditime
items.description: Digital credit optimization through research & journey mapping

items.title: Onda de Mar
items.description: Information architecture transformation for seamless product discovery

items.title: Malva
items.description: Unified brand experience across 16 stores and digital channels

achievements: Qualitative and quantitative research management (heatmaps, interviews, and surveys) for Kreditime, resulting in the acquisition of 1000+ potential buyers.
Experience architect in taking Kreditime to production; optimizing the sales funnel with a conversion increase from 15% to 20%.
Establishment of scalable Design Systems and multi-brand information architecture for global clients such as Malva, Onda de Mar, and Invicta.
Implementation of the Double Diamond methodology to optimize team efficiency and strategic planning via structured Notion documentation.
Led UI redesign for Invicta Mexico, focusing on optimizing checkout flows and a user-centered catalog organization to improve UX.
Creation of high-fidelity interactive wireframes and dynamic prototypes, ensuring visual and functional consistency through reusable component libraries.
Cross-functional collaboration in the Kreditime ecosystem under agile methodologies (Scrum) for rapid iteration on complex business requirements.
Execution of monthly usability and accessibility audits to ensure compliance with global e-commerce standards across active projects.
Design and development of interactive digital content for intellectual property education, improving the usability of learning platforms.
Conducting user research and behavior analysis to identify pedagogical needs and inform educational content design.
Collaboration with subject-matter experts and stakeholders to ensure successful launch of digital assets aligned with institutional objectives.
Expert use of Figma and Adobe Creative Suite for high-fidelity wireframes development and prototypes validated with end users.

kreditime.hero_description: I led the experience of a fintech product focused on activating buyers and bringing a credit solution to production with a focus on trust and clarity.
kreditime.context: I designed the Kreditime UX experience from research, flow definition, and coordinated work with development. Kreditime emerged as a bet to enable a clearer, more usable credit experience connected with eCommerce.
kreditime.problem: The challenge was not just building a financial interface, but designing a trustworthy experience for people interested in accessing credit without friction at the moment of purchase.
kreditime.role_description: I conducted research with heatmaps, interviews, and surveys to understand behavior, identifying critical pain points in the application process.
kreditime.process_description: The research allowed us to detect signals of interest, friction, and opportunity within the flow. We used heatmaps to visualize where users dropped off and why.
process_steps: Identification of bottlenecks in document uploading.
Validation of micro-copy to reduce financial anxiety.
Mapping the user journey from external checkout.
kreditime.results_description: The implementation of the new information architecture and KYC flow had a measurable impact on both user trust and business conversion.

metrics.label: Adoption
metrics.desc: We successfully got a thousand buyers to complete their profile thanks to the removal of redundant fields.

metrics.label: Conversion
metrics.desc: The seamless integration in the external checkout reduced cognitive load, allowing credit to be perceived as a facilitator.

metrics.label: UX Efficiency
metrics.desc: The drastic reduction in abandonment is attributed to the new real-time feedback system.

kreditime.learning_quote: In fintech, designing trust is just as important as designing usability.
kreditime.learning: In the fintech sector, development speed must be balanced with informational clarity. The user is not just looking for money; they are looking for the peace of mind of understanding their financial commitment.

ondademar.hero_title: Redesigning the future of Onda de Mar
ondademar.hero_description: Transformation of the information architecture for one of Colombia's most iconic fashion brands, improving navigability and conversion.
ondademar.context: Onda de Mar is an eCommerce platform for swimwear in LATAM. The existing structure required too many clicks to discover relevant products, generating high bounce rates.
ondademar.problem: Product discovery required excessive pagination. Weak categorization caused users to bounce without purchasing.
ondademar.role_description: I led and supervised the UX/UI team. Redesigned 15 pages, establishing a new information architecture.
ondademar.process_description: Applied double diamond methodology with user research and iterative testing to simplify navigation.
process_steps: Audit of 500+ SKUs to optimize taxonomy.
Card Sorting sessions with real users.
ondademar.results_description: The architecture transformation drastically reduced steps to purchase and increased session time.

metrics.label: Discovery
metrics.desc: Fewer pages required to find any product in the catalog.

metrics.label: Sales
metrics.desc: Direct correlation between improved search and purchase completion.

metrics.label: Retention
metrics.desc: Users now visit double the categories per session.

ondademar.learning_quote: Simplifying is not about removing elements, it's about adding clarity to the user's path.
ondademar.learning: eCommerce success is not about more features, but about reducing friction. When users find what they are looking for in half the steps, conversion follows naturally.

malva.hero_description: Omnichannel design strategy and scalable design system for 16 physical stores and luxury digital platforms.
malva.context: Malva operates 16 physical stores and needed a cohesive digital experience that reflected the luxury of its physical brand in the digital environment.
malva.problem: Disconnected digital and physical experiences. No unified design language. Store experience was not reflected digitally.
malva.role_description: I led Design Thinking sessions, established the design system, and coordinated omnichannel experience design.
malva.process_description: I created a complete library of components and design tokens to ensure consistency across all platforms.
malva.learning: Omnichannel isn't about having the same button in all places—it's about consistent logic, familiar patterns, and seamless transitions.
